persona.name: The High-Frequency Everything Spender
persona.tagline: Shops everywhere, often, and stays loyal month after month
persona.description: These 165 customers spend across all 14 categories at well above-average rates, with grocery net online orders running nearly twice the typical amount (1.94x) and food & dining visits hitting ~100 times a year. They log an average of 1,723 transactions annually and their total annual spend tops $73,700. Loyalty is strong — most categories show 15–17 consecutive months of activity.
traits: Shops online for groceries nearly twice as often as the average customer
Dines out roughly 100 times a year — almost twice a week
Fills up the tank or uses transport 136 times a year, spending ~$8,900
Loyal across the board — 15 to 17 consecutive active months in most categories
Spreads spending widely: all 14 categories are above average

persona.name: The Ghost Account Holder
persona.tagline: Has a bank account but barely uses it
persona.description: These 28 customers recorded zero spend and zero average transaction value, yet still generated about 10 transactions per year. This looks like an account that is open but essentially dormant — perhaps used only for tiny automated credits or internal transfers. There is nothing to distinguish their spending habits because there is virtually no spending.
traits: Zero dollars of recorded spend across the entire year
Only ~10 transactions per year — less than one per month
No high-value transactions whatsoever (0% high-value rate)
No loyalty signal in any spending category
Likely dormant or placeholder accounts rather than active consumers

persona.name: The Rare Big-Ticket Online Shopper
persona.tagline: Buys almost nothing — but when they do, it costs a lot
persona.description: These 20 customers make fewer than 10 transactions a year total, but each purchase is enormous — averaging $633 per transaction, with online shopping averaging nearly $900 per purchase and misc online items nearly $700. Almost 89% of their transactions qualify as high-value. They are not regular shoppers; they appear to make a handful of large, deliberate purchases online each year.
traits: Averages only ~10 transactions per year across all categories
Each transaction averages $633 — by far the highest of any cluster
Online shopping average of ~$894 per purchase suggests luxury or bulk buying
88.9% of transactions are classified as high-value
Almost no loyalty streak — sporadic, infrequent activity with less than 1 consecutive month in most categories

persona.name: The Heavy In-Store & Online Shopper with Personal Care Habit
persona.tagline: Shops hard in-store and online, and keeps up a strong personal care routine
persona.description: These 236 customers — the largest group at 24% — are prolific shoppers both in stores (131 shopping_pos transactions, $11,400/yr) and online (105 shopping_net transactions, $8,989/yr), both well above average. Personal care is also a standout at 1.52x average, with 100 visits per year. Their grocery_pos average transaction of $151 is unusually high, suggesting large basket sizes or a premium grocery preference.
traits: Visits physical shops 131 times a year, spending over $11,400 — 55% above average
Online shopping hits 105 transactions and nearly $9,000 per year
Personal care appointments or purchases number 100 times a year — 52% above average
Grocery basket is large: each grocery_pos trip averages $151
Strong loyalty in shopping and food channels — 16 to 17 consecutive active months

persona.name: The Moderate All-Around Spender with Low Online Grocery Use
persona.tagline: Spends at a steady pace across most categories but skips the online grocery habit
persona.description: These 183 customers sit comfortably below average across most categories, generating about 999 transactions per year — roughly half of what Clusters 0 and 3 produce. Online grocery (grocery_net) is strikingly low at only 0.36x average and just $1,047/yr, while most other categories hover around 0.75–0.88x average. They are consistent but restrained spenders who haven't shifted much purchasing online.
traits: Transaction volume of ~999/yr — roughly half the pace of the heaviest spenders
Online grocery use is very low: only 12 transactions a year at 0.36x average
Kids & pets and grocery in-store spending are the relative bright spots
Online shopping average transaction of $112 suggests occasional but deliberate online purchases
Loyal in most categories with 15–16 consecutive active months where they do engage

persona.name: The Frequent Driver & Gym-Goer Who Skips Online Grocery
persona.tagline: Fuels up constantly and hits the gym regularly, but barely shops for groceries online
persona.description: These 222 customers stand out for their heavy gas and transport use — 129 transactions and $7,722/yr, 35% above average — paired with above-average health and fitness spending. But online grocery use (grocery_net) collapses to just 0.27x average, suggesting they prefer in-store shopping or simply don't use delivery services. Their overall spending is moderate, around $59,000/yr total.
traits: Gas and transport is the defining habit: 129 transactions/yr at 1.35x average
Health and fitness visits number 71 per year — 15% above average
Online grocery almost absent: only 9 transactions/yr, 0.27x the average
In-store grocery spending is high per transaction ($133) but frequency is near average
Travel spending is modestly above average ($87/trip), hinting at occasional getaways

persona.name: The Extremely Rare Large-Purchase Account (Near-Dormant)
persona.tagline: Almost never transacts, but each purchase is a major spend
persona.description: These 27 customers are nearly identical to Cluster 2 in behaviour — very few transactions (fewer than 10 per year) with very high per-transaction values, especially in online shopping (~$974/transaction) and in-store shopping (~$731/transaction). Unlike Cluster 2, this group has an even lower transaction count and slightly lower average spend per transaction ($601 vs $633), and shows traces of activity in more categories. Both are infrequent big-ticket buyers, but this group is marginally more scattered in where they do spend.
traits: Fewer than 10 transactions per year — account is nearly inactive
Online shopping averages $974 per purchase — suggests very high-end or bulk orders
83% of transactions are high-value, second only to Cluster 2
No consecutive loyalty months to speak of — purely sporadic activity
Distinguished from Cluster 2 by slightly more category breadth and lower avg transaction value

persona.name: The Home-Focused Traveller with Big Grocery & Shopping Baskets
persona.tagline: Spends big on home, travel, and shopping but doesn't drive much
persona.description: These 102 customers have a striking travel spend of $15,151/yr at an average of $467 per travel transaction — far above any other cluster — suggesting premium or long-haul trips. They also spend heavily at home ($8,239/yr) and in grocery stores ($10,754/yr). However, gas and transport is well below average (0.39x), which is unusual for high-spenders and may indicate urban living or infrequent commuting.
traits: Travel spend averages $467 per transaction — the highest travel spend in any cluster
Annual travel total of $15,151 suggests premium or frequent long-distance trips
Home spending of $8,239/yr across 104 transactions points to active home improvement or furnishing
Gas and transport is only 0.39x average — unusually low for an otherwise high-spending group
36.6% high-value transaction rate — the highest among the large active clusters
